title: Calculator Registry
description: The canonical, static list of Calculator Registry descriptors — Phase 2B Batch PXP-6 (docs/44-PHASE-2B-TECHNICAL-PLAN.md §8/§17/§22, ADR-013, ADR-012, docs/47-PHASE-2B-IMPLEMENTATION-RULES.md). Phase 2B's Pillar 3 (docs/44 §4.1) — the platform's only mechanism for expressing which deterministic, doctor/staff-authored calculators exist at all, mirroring shared/constants/module-registry.json's 'availability, not enablement' framing and shared/constants/template-registry.json's 'shape, not capability' framing. Each entry is a versioned CalculatorDefinition (docs/44 §8.2): calculator_slug/version identify it, input_fields declares the exact shape a CalculatorResult.input_snapshot must validate against (mirroring template-registry.json's questions convention), formula_reference is a descriptive pointer only — no executable formula logic is stored or run anywhere in this batch (docs/44 §8.3's 'never hardcoded per disease' constraint, ADR-013's 'deterministic, doctor-authored, never AI-computed' constraint: a formula's actual computation happens client-side or in a future definition-execution batch, never in this generic registry/storage layer). Deliberately seeded empty in this batch: no concrete calculator (disease-specific or otherwise) is registered here — Calculator Registry batch PXP-6 delivers only the generic, pluggable registry-and-result mechanism itself; a future calculator is added as its own registry entry by whichever later, separately-approved batch actually designs and authors it (docs/47 §4's 'a new module/calculator/template is a new registry entry, never new architecture'), the same discipline module-registry.json's own header comment already established for its own not-yet-built capabilities. apps-script/CalculatorRegistry.gs hand-ports this list into its own Foundation-prefixed array, the same convention module-registry.json's and template-registry.json's own consumers already use. See calculator-registry.md for the full field-by-field explanation and the disclosed 'ships empty' scope decision.
